children.heading: Closure or close
children.label: 1 Closure or close
children.text: The term “closure or close” means the termination of operations at open dumps on Indian land or Alaska Native land and bringing such dumps into compliance with applicable Federal standards and regulations, or standards promulgated by an Indian tribal government or Alaska Native entity, if such standards are more stringent than the Federal standards and regulations.

children.heading: Director
children.label: 2 Director
children.text: The term “Director” means the Director of the Indian Health Service.

children.label: A
children.text: land within the limits of any Indian reservation under the jurisdiction of the United States Government, notwithstanding the issuance of any patent, and including rights-of-way running through the reservation;

children.label: B
children.text: dependent Indian communities within the borders of the United States whether within the original or subsequently acquired territory thereof, and whether within or without the limits of a State; and

children.label: C
children.text: Indian allotments, the Indian titles to which have not been extinguished, including rights-of-way running through such allotments.

children.heading: Alaska Native land
children.label: 4 Alaska Native land
children.text: The term “Alaska Native land” means (A) land conveyed or to be conveyed pursuant to the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act [ et seq.], including any land reconveyed under section 14(c)(3) of that Act (), and (B) land conveyed pursuant to the Act of ( et seq.; commonly known as the “Fur Seal Act of 1966”).43 U.S.C. 1601143 U.S.C. 1613(c)(3)16 U.S.C. 1151See References in Text note below.1November 2, 1966

children.heading: Indian tribal government
children.label: 5 Indian tribal government
children.text: The term “Indian tribal government” means the governing body of any Indian tribe, band, nation, pueblo, or other organized group or community which is recognized as eligible for the special programs and services provided by the United States to Indians because of their status as Indians.

children.heading: Alaska Native entity
children.label: 6 Alaska Native entity
children.text: The term “Alaska Native entity” includes native corporations established pursuant to the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act [ et seq.] and any Alaska Native village or municipal entity which owns Alaska Native land.43 U.S.C. 1601

children.heading: Open dump
children.label: 7 Open dump
children.text: The term “open dump” means any facility or site where solid waste is disposed of which is not a sanitary landfill which meets the criteria promulgated under section 4004 of the Solid Waste Disposal Act () and which is not a facility for disposal of hazardous waste.42 U.S.C. 6944

children.heading: Postclosure maintenance
children.label: 8 Postclosure maintenance
children.text: The term “postclosure maintenance” means any activity undertaken at a closed solid waste management facility on Indian land or on Alaska Native land to maintain the integrity of containment features, monitor compliance with applicable performance standards, or remedy any situation or occurrence that violates regulations promulgated pursuant to subtitle D of the Solid Waste Disposal Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 6941

children.heading: Service
children.label: 9 Service
children.text: The term “Service” means the Indian Health Service.

children.heading: Solid waste
children.label: 10 Solid waste
children.text: The term “solid waste” has the meaning provided that term by section 1004(27) of the Solid Waste Disposal Act () and any regulations promulgated thereunder.42 U.S.C. 6903
